description_long: This is a small, three-legged wooden stool with a semi-circular seat made from light-colored, natural wood. The seat surface displays visible wood grain patterns, giving it a rustic and organic appearance. Each of the three legs is angled outward for stability, with two legs made from a darker wood and one from a lighter wood, creating a visually striking contrast. The legs are solid and rectangular in cross-section, attached directly to the underside of the seat without any visible fasteners or decorative joinery. The stool's design is simple and functional, with no backrest or footrest, emphasizing minimalism and practicality. Its compact size makes it suitable for use in urban parks, sidewalks, or public waiting areas.
description: A compact, three-legged wooden stool with a semi-circular light wood seat and contrasting dark and light wood legs. The design is minimalist and functional, with visible wood grain and no decorative elements.
description_view_0: The stool is seen from an angle highlighting the rounded edge of the seat and the contrasting dark and light wood legs.
description_view_1: This view shows the flat edge of the seat and the outward splay of the three legs, emphasizing the stool's stability.
description_view_2: The stool is viewed from the opposite side, showing the seat's wood grain and the arrangement of the legs, with the dark leg most prominent.
description_view_3: A direct view of the seat's flat edge, with all three legs visible and the color contrast between the legs most apparent.
